title: Flexell Space raises $20 Million in Series A round
summary: Flexell Space, a developer of next-generation space solar solutions, raised $20 million in a Series A funding round. The company is based in Uiwang, South Korea.

title: Defense spending lifts Europe’s space economy
summary: European government space spending increased by 12% to $15.4 billion in 2025, driven by rising national defense budgets, despite a 3% global decline.

title: L3Harris, Sierra Space to build 36 satellites to expand U.S. missile-tracking network
summary: L3Harris and Sierra Space have been awarded a combined $1.75 billion contract by the Space Development Agency to build 36 satellites for expanding the U.S. missile-tracking network.

title: Poland to host new ESA center as government boosts investments in space
summary: Poland will host the first European Space Agency (ESA) center in an eastern flank member state, focusing on civil security and resilience. The move aligns with Poland's increased investments in the space sector.

title: Accel and Prosus backed QOSMIC raises $3.33 Million to Build Optical Ground Stations for the Orbital Data Economy
summary: QOSMIC raised $3.33 million in seed funding led by Accel and Prosus to develop optical ground stations for the orbital data economy. The Bengaluru-based company aims to support high-speed data transmission from space.

title: Reditus Space completes first reentry vehicle
summary: Reditus Space, a startup focused on recoverable and reusable spacecraft, has completed its first reentry vehicle. The vehicle is scheduled for launch later this year.

title: Voyager completes acquisition of Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Technologies has finalized its acquisition of Astrobotic Technology, a lunar infrastructure firm. Astrobotic recently secured two NASA lunar lander missions.

title: Getting ready for lunar planetary defense
summary: NASA has unveiled plans for a new moon base, marking a significant step toward establishing a permanent human presence on the Moon. This development highlights the growing momentum in space exploration and the push for lunar planetary defense.

title: China lines up methalox Long March 10C as commercial workhorse after first booster recovery
summary: China has designated the methalox Long March 10C as a commercial workhorse after successfully recovering its first booster. The move follows an injection of fresh capital into commercial rocket ventures.

title: SpaceX gears up for Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX is preparing for the 13th flight of its Starship rocket, scheduled for July 16, aiming to test fixes from the previous flight and deploy operational Starlink satellites.

title: SpaceX Starship Flight 13 launch updates: SpaceX sets target date for next Starship rocket launch
summary: SpaceX has set a target date for the next launch of its Starship rocket, marking another step in its testing program. The company continues to advance toward the 13th flight of the Starship system.

title: Russia's launching a NASA astronaut and 2 cosmonauts to the International Space Station on July 14: Watch it live
summary: Russia is launching a NASA astronaut and two cosmonauts to the International Space Station on July 14. The event will be broadcast live.

title: These mysterious exoplanets may have clouds of vaporized rock and grounds of scorching magma oceans
summary: Sub-Neptune exoplanets may have atmospheres dense enough to vaporize rock, forming clouds and trapping heat above scorching magma oceans. This discovery sheds light on the extreme conditions of these mysterious planets.

title: NASA begins stacking rocket ahead of 2027 Artemis III astronaut launch (photos)
summary: NASA has started assembling the solid rocket booster segments for the SLS rocket, which will launch astronauts on the Artemis III mission. The Artemis III mission is currently scheduled for 2027.

title: SpaceX targets July 16 for Starship Flight 13, reveals what went wrong on previous launch
summary: SpaceX has scheduled Starship Flight 13 for no earlier than July 16, following the mostly successful Flight 12. The company also shared details on what went wrong during the previous launch.

title: "The Ninth Jedi" is a bold new Star Wars anime project hitting Disney+ next month, and it looks sick!
summary: A new Star Wars anime project titled 'The Ninth Jedi' will premiere on Disney+ next month, expanding on a 2021 'Star Wars: Visions' episode directed by Kenji Kamiyama.

title: Extremely Large Telescope reaches a major milestone | Space photo of the day for July 13, 2026
summary: The Extremely Large Telescope has achieved a significant milestone, as highlighted in a space photo featured on July 13, 2026. The image showcases the telescope's impressive scale.

title: Getting Vikram-1 to orbit: Inside Skyroot Aerospace's coming bid to make spaceflight history
summary: Skyroot Aerospace is preparing to launch its Vikram-1 rocket, aiming to revolutionize India's commercial space industry with a valuation of $1.1 billion. The upcoming flight of Vikram-1 is expected to mark a historic milestone for the company.

title: Space junk debris cloud discovered in high-traffic orbit 'is a potential minefield' for the costliest satellites
summary: A debris cloud in geosynchronous orbit poses a significant threat to expensive satellites, described as a 'potential minefield'. This highlights the growing risks of space debris in high-traffic orbital regions.

title: The FCC just gave Reflect Orbital permission to launch its 1st space mirror to orbit. Tens of thousands more could follow
summary: The FCC approved Reflect Orbital to launch its first space mirror into orbit later this year, with plans for tens of thousands more in the next decade.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4941-4947: (Pin)Stripes on the Fourth of July
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is approaching a geologic boundary on Mars ahead of the Fourth of July holiday, observing polygonal patterns in the terrain. The rover's operations are led by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory.

title: NASA Study Points to Smoother Air Taxi Rides
summary: NASA research aims to improve passenger comfort in air taxis, a key factor in the emerging eVTOL industry. The study could help shape ride planning to enhance willingness to fly.

title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon is set to launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on July 14, 2026, alongside cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina. This mission will mark Menon's first spaceflight as part of Expedition.

title: NASA’s Hubble Discovers First of Star Cluster’s Missing Black Holes
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope, with supporting data from Webb, has discovered the first stellar-mass black hole in the star cluster Omega Centauri. This marks a significant finding in the search for missing black holes within star clusters.

title: Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards
summary: NASA's Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards will fund advanced research in spacecraft atmospheric entry diagnostics and autonomous navigation using machine learning. The awards support innovative methods for onboard guidance, navigation, and control in high-enthalpy test facilities.

title: NASA Volunteers Help Zooniverse Reach 1 Billion Classifications
summary: NASA volunteers have helped Zooniverse, a NASA grantee, reach 1 billion classifications through their online research platform. This milestone highlights the contributions of global volunteers in analyzing scientific data.

title: NASA Photographer Captures Images from F-18 Over Washington
summary: NASA photographers captured unique aerial images of the agency's missions from an F-18 over Washington. These photos provide rare behind-the-scenes views of NASA's research and operations.

title: Waxing Gibbous Moon
summary: The image shows the waxing gibbous moon captured from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. The space station was 264 miles above the Indian Ocean southeast of Madagascar at the time.

title: Where Venezuela’s Earthquakes Shifted the Ground
summary: Radar data from NASA's NISAR satellite revealed significant ground displacement in La Guaira and nearby areas of Venezuela due to the June 2026 earthquakes. This highlights the satellite's capability to monitor seismic activity and its impacts.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are scheduled worldwide this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key step before its planned launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20's 33 engines, marking a significant milestone in the company's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being developed for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, marking a continued revitalization of the region's space industry. Multiple launch providers are advancing their facilities at Cape Canaveral.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved several key milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital destination ahead of future larger habitats.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is advancing preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are accelerating efforts to meet this goal.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. This move aims to support future lunar exploration and base construction efforts.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral following a recent anomaly that damaged the facility. The company is implementing a hybrid plan to restore operations.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years of achievements with a commemorative booklet highlighting 50 hallmark accomplishments. The publication reflects on five decades of space exploration and innovation.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 ministerial meeting, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities. The events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential applications. It emphasizes the innovation and technological advancements in space robotics.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The article discusses the importance of sensitivity and sturdiness in space industry components. It highlights the need for robust systems to handle sensitive operations in harsh environments.

title: Reditus Finishes ENOS Reentry Vehicle Ahead of First Flight
summary: Reditus has completed the development of its first reentry spacecraft, ENOS, ahead of its planned debut flight this fall.

title: Reflect Orbital Cleared to Fly In-Orbit Mirror by FCC
summary: Reflect Orbital has received FCC clearance to launch an in-orbit mirror. The FCC stated that concerns about the spacecraft's environmental and astronomical impact are beyond its jurisdiction.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for beaming power to satellites in orbit. The funding will advance their mission to enhance satellite capabilities through in-space power transmission.

title: US Government Studying Offshore Launch Opportunities
summary: The US Government is exploring offshore launch opportunities by studying the potential for building or repurposing infrastructure on the Outer Continental Shelf for space launch and reentry. The Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) issued a Request for Information (RFI) to gather relevant data.

title: NATO Allies Plan to Stitch Together Sovereign Space Fleets
summary: Eight NATO allies announced plans to integrate their national defense satellites into a unified network. This initiative aims to enhance collective space-based defense capabilities among member states.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
summary: Blue Origin is seeking $10 billion in funding at a $130 billion valuation, marking a shift from Jeff Bezos' personal funding. The company is reportedly moving towards a more independent financial structure.

title: Auxilium Demos Tech To Enable Sustainable Bioprinting in Orbit
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies successfully created multiple types of organ tissues aboard the ISS, demonstrating scalable bioprinting technology in orbit. This achievement highlights potential future applications for sustainable bioprinting in space.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: ispace has secured 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for a lunar mission launching no earlier than 2030. This marks the company's expansion into a new lunar integrator service.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B to Scale RDRE Production
summary: Venus Aerospace has secured $91 million in Series B funding to expand the production of its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine (RDRE) technology. The investment highlights a growing industry trend toward more efficient rocket propulsion systems.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to vote on the Space Modernization Order on July 22, aiming to reform satellite licensing processes. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced this initiative last week.

title: L3Harris and Sierra Space Receive $1.75B for SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer
summary: L3Harris Technologies and Sierra Space secured $1.75 billion in contracts for the SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer satellites. The funds will enable an expedited launch under fixed price OTA rapid prototyping contracts.

title: The Implications of Dual-Use for Protecting Space Assets
summary: The article discusses the evolving implications of dual-use capabilities in Europe's space sector, where traditional distinctions between civil, defense, commercial, and government roles are blurring. It highlights the challenges of protecting space assets amid these shifting dynamics.

title: Ravee Optics Secures $6 Million Seed Round to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics, a startup developing compact laser terminals, has secured $6 million in an oversubscribed seed funding round. The funds will be used to advance the development of their laser communication technology.

title: Sonatel Taps Eutelsat OneWeb for Teleport Upgrade
summary: Sonatel, Senegal's leading telecommunications company, has completed a teleport upgrade in collaboration with Eutelsat OneWeb. The upgrade aims to enhance connectivity services for the country's population of over 19 million people.

title: FCC Approves Reflect Orbital Demo Satellite to Test Redirecting Sunlight
summary: The FCC has approved Reflect Orbital's demonstration satellite to test redirecting sunlight from orbit back to Earth. This marks a significant step in solar reflector technology for potential applications in energy and climate research.

title: Space42 and Skylo Test D2D Test on Thuraya-4
summary: Space42 and Skylo successfully demonstrated direct-to-device (D2D) testing on the Thuraya-4 satellite, including SMS and SOS messaging. The partners announced the test results on July 9 and expect to roll out further services.

title: Ovzon’s GEO Connectivity and Terminals Complete Arctic Tests
summary: Ovzon has successfully completed Arctic trials to test its GEO connectivity and terminals in extreme environments. The company conducted three High North expeditions to validate its technology.

title: Simera Sense to Supply Imagers for Canary Islands Satellite Constellation
summary: Simera Sense will supply multispectral imagers for the Canary Islands satellite constellation project. Telespazio Ibérica has selected Simera Sense for this role.

title: MDA Space Vertically Integrates in EO Analytics With Deal to Acquire Majority of CLS
summary: MDA Space is acquiring a majority stake in French Earth observation analytics company CLS to vertically integrate its EO capabilities. The deal strengthens MDA Space's position in the Earth observation data analytics market.

title: SpaceX Files for 100,000-Satellite Gen-3 Constellation
summary: SpaceX has filed with the FCC to launch a Gen3 constellation of 100,000 satellites, aiming to provide multi-gigabit connectivity. The filing includes plans to use W- and D-band frequencies.
